D3 Chemistry L4 Analyze Reference

Conjugated π Chain — Longer = Redder

in a chain of alternating single and double bonds, the HOMO-LUMO gap shrinks as the chain grows; at ~11 conjugated double bonds the absorption falls into the visible — β-carotene is orange

A π-conjugated chain is a one-dimensional box for the bonding electrons. As the chain gets longer the allowed orbital energies pack closer; the HOMO-LUMO gap shrinks; the absorption wavelength shifts toward the red. Around N=10–11 conjugated double bonds the absorption enters the visible — this is why carotenoids are orange/red.

Drag N (number of conjugated double bonds). The chain length grows; the predicted λ_max moves; absorption band overlays the visible spectrum. Anchor markers: β-carotene N=11, lycopene N=11, retinal N=5.
